person.name: William Murray of Sutherland
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
William, son of Freskin, appears on the Scottish record in the latter half of the 12 Century. His first appearance, as William, son of Freskin was as a witness to a charter granted at Perth by King Malcolm IV. in 1160 to Berowald the Fleming of the lands of Innes, in Morayshire. William must have been of age at this point and was likely born c. 1130.

Between 1166 and 1171 he appears in a charter by King William the Lion, which confirms the lands, named as having been held by Freskin. It seems likely, and has been assumed, that Freskin died shortly before and thus c. 1165. Sir William Fraser makes the point that "this charter by King William the Lion must be said to be the foundation charter of the Sutherland family."

He seems to have lived in the vicinity of Elgin as he signs a number of Royal Charters there. The date of his grant to the Lordship of Duffus is not clear but he appears with that title after 1187.

Balfour Paul asserts that "He seems to have survived the year 1204, if he were the William Fresekyn who was Sheriff of Invernaryn in that year". If this is so he must have been of some age for the time and presumably did not live long after.

Married Ada UNKNOWN, daughter of Malise, Seneschal of Strathern. He acquired the lands of Tullibardine, or which his descendants were barons.

«b»Family«/b»
The name of his wife is not provided in source. 

«b»He is known to have had three sons:«/b»
1.) Hugh. Hugh will become the major beneficiary of the estates.

2.) William. He is seen, on record, as William, son of William, son of Freskin. He and his brother, Hugh, frequently appear together as witnesses after 1195. He also, about 1200 or later, assumed the surname "de Moravia", and in a charter about that date refers to Hugh as "his lord and brother", which proves the latter's seniority. He became lord of Petty and Bracholy, Boharm and Arteldol, and died before 1226. He is believed to be the ancestor of the Morays of Bothwell.

3.) Andrew. He joined Holy Orders and is described in a writ of date before 1203 as son of William, son of Freskin, and parson of Duffus. He is also described in a later writ by Hugh Freskin as brother to him and William. He is named in 1221, but it is not certain that he was alive at that time.

zh.lifeSummary: 當William Murray of Sutherland於1138年出生在蘇格蘭艾爾金爾度福斯時，他的父親Flemming Freskin de Moray32歲，母親Macheth McKay27歲。他於1159年與Deolifard Oliphant在英國蘇格蘭莫雷度福斯結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於1203年在他的家鄉享年65歲。

nameMeanings.name: Murray
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Moray in northeastern Scotland, which derives from Celtic mori- ‘sea’ + treb- ‘settlement’. The founder of the Scottish house of Murray was a Fleming named Freskin who was granted Strathbrock in West Lothian and Duffus in Moray by David I. The family took its name from the region in the late 12th century.
Irish and Scottish: shortened form of   McMurray  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Muireadhaigh ‘descendant of Muireadhach’ a personal name meaning ‘mariner’. Occasionally it may be a shortened form of McMurray .

variants.name: Murri
meanings: Probably Scottish: variant of   Murray  .

variants.name: Murry
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   Murray  .

variants.name: Murrah
meanings: Probably an altered form of Scottish   Murray  .

variants.name: Murie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel.
Scottish: habitational name from Murie in Errol (Perthshire).
Scottish and Irish: variant of   Murray  .

variants.name: Merhi
meanings: Arabic (mainly Lebanon and Syria): from a variant of the personal name Mirhi of unknown meaning. Bearers of this surname are both Muslims and Christians. Compare Murray 5.

variants.name: Murrey
meanings: Irish: variant of   Murray  .

variants.name: McMurray
meanings: Irish (northern) and Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Muireadhaigh or Mac Muireadhaich, a patronymic from the personal name Muireadhach ‘seafarer’ (see Murdoch ). Compare McCurry 1.

variants.name: Murra
meanings: Italian (Sicily): topographic name from Sicilian murra ‘large rock, mass of rocks’ or a habitational name from a place called with this word.
East Frisian: unexplained.
Albanian: nickname from murrë (definite form murra) ‘dark gray cow’.

variants.name: Murrie
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Murray  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: Sutherland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Caithness): habitational name from the Scottish county of Sutherland, named with Old Norse súthr ‘south, southern’ + land ‘land’ because the territory lay south of Scandinavia and the Norse colonies in the Orkney and Shetland Islands.
History: This is the name of a Highland Scottish clan associated with the county of Sutherland.

variants.name: Southerland
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Sutherland  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Murray of Sutherland was born in 1138, in Duffus, Elginshire, Scotland, his father, Flemming Freskin de Moray, was 32 and his mother, Macheth McKay, was 27. He married Deolifard Oliphant in 1159, in Duffus, Moray, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died in 1203, in his hometown, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Murray of Sutherland est né en 1138, à Duffus, Elginshire, Écosse, son père, Flemming Freskin de Moray, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Macheth McKay, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Deolifard Oliphant en 1159, à Duffus, Moray, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Il est décédé en 1203, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 65 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Murray of Sutherland 1138 in Duffus, Elginshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Flemming Freskin de Moray 32 und seine Mutter Macheth McKay 27. Er hat 1159 in Duffus, Moray, Schottland, United Kingdom Deolifard Oliphant geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. Er starb 1203 im Alter von 65 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Murray of Sutherland è nato il nel 1138 nel Duffus, Elginshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Flemming Freskin de Moray, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Macheth McKay, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Deolifard Oliphant nel 1159, nel Duffus, Moray, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli. Morì nel 1203, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 65 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William Murray of Sutherlandが1138年にスコットランドエルジンシュアイアダフウスで生まれた時，父Flemming Freskin de Morayは32歳，母Macheth McKayは27歳であった。1159年にイギリススコットランドモーアイダフウスでDeolifard Oliphantと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子の親であった。彼は1203年に故郷で65歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William Murray of Sutherland(이)가 1138년 스컷랜드 엘긴실르 더프스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Flemming Freskin de Moray(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Macheth McKay(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1159년 영국 스코틀랜드 모라이 더프스에서 Deolifard Oliphant(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1203년 그의 고향에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Murray of Sutherland nasceu em 1138, em Duffus, Elginshire, Scotland, seu pai, Flemming Freskin de Moray, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Macheth McKay, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Deolifard Oliphant em 1159, em Duffus, Moray, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1203, em sua cidade natal, com 65 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Murray of Sutherland родился в 1138 г. в местности дуффус, элгиншир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Flemming Freskin de Moray было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Macheth McKay, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Deolifard Oliphant в 1159 г. в местности дуффус, мореи, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Он умер в 1203 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 65 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Murray of Sutherland nació en 1138, en Duffus, Elginshire, Scotland, su papá, Flemming Freskin de Moray, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Macheth McKay, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Deolifard Oliphant en 1159, en Duffus, Moray, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos. Murió en 1203, en su pueblo, a la edad de 65 años.
